methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Waymo, a pre-IPO AI & Software company founded by Sebastian Thrun and Anthony Levandowski, is valued at $126B following $16B+ in total capital raised. The relevant peer universe consists of 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and 8 private peers spanning the AI & Software sector, including Figure AI, Applied Intuition, Skild AI, and Crusoe Energy.

Peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable to this engagement. The AI & Software sector contains 12 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for companies at matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier yielded fewer than 3 datapoints in each narrow category—below the minimum threshold for reliable triangulation. A broader all-sector median would risk introducing material distortion given Waymo's specialized autonomous systems positioning and capital intensity relative to generalist software comparables.

The valuation assessment therefore depends on alternative methods not detailed in the current dataset. Key uncertainties include (1) the appropriate discount or premium to apply relative to the single public comparable given stage and sector dynamics, (2) revenue run-rate and profitability trajectory relative to peer benchmarks, and (3) the liquidity discount warranted for a pre-IPO position. Further analysis incorporating revenue multiples, DCF assumptions, or precedent transaction data would be required to triangulate a defensible range around the current $126B valuation.
